blockers.description: The prompt asks for "elements usually found in inorganic compounds", and "elements usually found in organic compounds", which makes it unclear which elements can be considered as such
example_questions: Which elements should I consider as usually found in inorganic or organic compounds?
Which are the elements usually found in inorganic or organic compounds?
Which elements do you refer as usually found in inorganic or organic compounds?
blockers.resolution: The elements usually found in inorganic compounds are: na, sn, pb, zn, cl, br and i, and the elements usually found in inorganic compounds are: c, h, o, n, s, p, and f

blockers.description: The prompt mentions a "high rate", but it is not explained what is considered as high exactly, regardless of which elements are organic or inorganic. It is missing information from the business information.
example_questions: What would a "high rate" of elements usually found in inorganic compounds vs elements usually found in organic compounds be?
What value does a "high rate" of elements usually found in inorganic compounds vs elements usually found in organic compounds refer to?
What should I consider as a "high rate" of elements usually found in inorganic compounds vs elements usually found in organic compounds?
blockers.resolution: A "high rate" of elements usually found in inorganic compounds vs elements usually found in organic compounds is a rate higher than 0.6.

blockers.description: The "tox_grade" column in the "molecule" table contains values that are numeric and cannot be interpreted as whether a molecule is safe or not. It is impossible to understand how to determine if a molecule is safe or not.
example_questions: How should I interpret the values from the "tox_grade" column in the "molecule" table?
What do the values from the "tox_grade" column in the "molecule" table mean?
How do I interpret the values from the "tox_grade" column in the "molecule" table?
blockers.resolution: A molecule is safe if the "tox_grade" column in the "molecule" table has the values "L1", "L2", or "L3"
